Variant B is substantially more useful for me as the operator of Śliwka w Kompot. While both reports correctly identify the seasonal, two-tiered wage market, Variant B provides a far more granular and actionable level of detail that reflects the true operational realities of running a restaurant on "Monciak." Its specificity is its greatest strength; by naming my direct competitors (ATELIER Sopot, SLICE & ICE) and their identical pay bands, it gives me a precise benchmark. More importantly, it identifies a critical, non-obvious bottleneck—the sparse late-night SKM train service impacting closing shifts—which is a genuine daily headache. This demonstrates a deep, synthesized understanding of the local context, connecting wage data to real estate prices, commuter patterns, and university schedules (the "May/June Gap"). Variant A is a good summary, but Variant B provides the kind of specific, high-signal intelligence that I can use to make immediate staffing and operational decisions.
